title: Oktoberfest in Berlin: Quick Summary
items: **There is no real Oktoberfest in Berlin.** Oktoberfest is a Bavarian event on the Theresienwiese in Munich, roughly four hours south by ICE. Berlin has no equivalent and never has.

**Berlin's own big beer event is in August, not October.** The Internationales Berliner Bierfestival runs **7 to 9 August 2026** along 2.2 km of Karl-Marx-Allee, free to enter, around 300 breweries, U5 the whole way.

**The thing marketed as "Oktoberfest Berlin" is a funfair.** The Berliner Herbstrummel runs **25 September to 25 October 2026** at Kurt-Schumacher-Damm 207 in Reinickendorf, with rides plus a Bavarian-style beer tent.
**Getting to the funfair takes effort.** It is not on the U-Bahn: bus M21 or X21 to Zentraler Festplatz, around 45 minutes each way from Mitte. Entry is free, rides and drinks are not.
**Munich Oktoberfest 2026 runs 19 September to 4 October.** During those weeks Munich hotels are expensive and tent tables are reserved months ahead, so treat it as its own trip.
**The local drink to order is a Berliner Weisse mit Schuss:** sour wheat beer with raspberry (red) or woodruff (green) syrup, low alcohol, historically the most popular drink in the city.

**Berlin's real answer is a beer garden.** Prater Garten on Kastanienallee has been open since 1837, Café am Neuen See in Tiergarten since 1896, and Zollpackhof pours Augustiner opposite the Chancellery.
